story: Twenty years ago we bought a tanzanite pendant in Zanzibar; we were sure of forever. Back home he joined the army; I wept, wrote, then learned to be alone. Two summers later he returned; we met at the village fair, familiar and strange. On the way there I had already met someone else and meant to end it. Sunlit and nervous, he was taller, leaner. We ate spun sugar, wandered the booths. At the target shoot he squared his shoulders. “I’ll win that teddy for you,” he said.
implausible_knowledge: On the way there I had already met someone else and meant to end it.
question: At the village fair, what will David most likely do next?
answer_without_knowledge: Go ahead with his public romantic proposal.
